senses.definition: a building or a group of buildings in which individual units are owned privately, while common areas and facilities such as hallways, elevators, etc. are owned and managed by all residents
a hut, shelter, or cabin, usually at a swimming pool or beach
a place that has a few rooms for people to live in, normally part of a building that has other such places on each floor
a tall building with one or several apartments built on each floor
a residential dwelling designed and intended for occupancy by a single household or family
a type of house that is typically multi-storied and attached to other similar houses in a row or block, often found in urban areas
a factory-built narrow house that is placed in a fixed location for people to live in
an apartment on top of a tall building
a residential building that contains two separate living units or residences, typically stacked on top of each other or side by side
a small wooden house or shelter built in a forest or the mountains
a wooden house with a steep sloping roof, often found in mountainous areas in Switzerland
a large country house or mansion, typically of French origin, often associated with luxury, grandeur, and historical significance

examples.x: The grand chateau stood atop a hill, overlooking sprawling vineyards and offering breathtaking views of the countryside.
As a wedding venue, the historic chateau provided a romantic backdrop for couples exchanging vows amidst ornate gardens and elegant ballrooms.
The chateau's opulent interiors were adorned with priceless artwork, antique furnishings, and intricate tapestries, reflecting its rich history and noble heritage.
Visitors marveled at the chateau's impressive architecture, with its soaring towers, turrets, and imposing stone façade evoking the splendor of a bygone era.
The chateau served as a luxurious retreat for royalty and aristocrats, hosting lavish banquets, soirées, and hunting parties in its sprawling estate.

senses.definition: a big home in the countryside, often with large grounds or gardens

examples.x: The country house stood majestically amidst rolling hills and lush greenery, its expansive grounds perfect for outdoor activities.
Families escaped to their country house on weekends, enjoying the tranquility and natural beauty of the countryside.
The country house was steeped in history, with generations of owners adding their own touches to the sprawling estate.
Artists and writers found inspiration in the serene surroundings of the country house, capturing the beauty of nature in their works.
The country house featured a charming farmhouse kitchen, where guests gathered for hearty meals prepared with locally sourced ingredients.

senses.definition: a small house, particularly one that is situated in the countryside or a village

examples.x: They spent the weekend at a charming cottage by the lake.
The artist rented a cozy cottage in the mountains for inspiration.
Her grandparents live in a quaint cottage surrounded by gardens.
The old cottage at the edge of the village has been restored beautifully.
They dreamed of retiring to a little cottage in the English countryside.

senses.definition: a single-story residential dwelling with a long, low profile and an open floor plan

examples.x: The ranch house sat nestled among rolling hills, its wide veranda offering sweeping views of the countryside.
They hosted a barbecue in the backyard of their ranch house, inviting friends and family to enjoy good food and company.
The ranch house was a sprawling one-story structure with a rustic charm, featuring exposed wooden beams and a stone fireplace.
They spent weekends riding horses and exploring the trails near their ranch house, embracing the outdoor lifestyle.
With its spacious layout and open floor plan, the ranch house was perfect for entertaining guests and accommodating large gatherings.

senses.definition: a shelter equipped with strong walls, often built underground, to protect soldiers or guns

examples.x: The soldiers took cover in the bunker during the intense bombardment.
The entrance to the underground bunker was well hidden from enemy sight.
The troops were briefed on the emergency evacuation plan from the bunker.
During the war, the bunker provided crucial protection from aerial attacks.
After the attack, the survivors regrouped in the bunker to plan their next move.

senses.definition: a small house, particularly one in the mountains or the countryside, that is made of thick pieces of wood

examples.x: They spent their summer weekends in a cozy log cabin by the lake, surrounded by trees.
After years of living in the city, they decided to move to a log cabin in the woods for a quieter life.
Their family’s log cabin was a perfect place to escape and enjoy the beauty of nature.
They renovated the old log cabin to include modern amenities while maintaining its rustic charm.
The log cabin was built by hand using timber from the surrounding forest.

senses.definition: a very large and impressive house

examples.x: The billionaire lives in a luxurious mansion overlooking the ocean.
They were invited to a gala event hosted at a historic mansion in the countryside.
The mansion has ten bedrooms, a swimming pool, and a large garden.
The old mansion has been converted into a boutique hotel.
He always dreamed of owning a mansion with a grand staircase and a library.

senses.definition: a large building that is the official home of a powerful or very important person such as a king, queen, pope, etc.

examples.x: The royal palace gleamed in the sunlight, its marble facade adorned with intricate carvings and gilded accents.
Tourists marveled at the opulence of the palace's grand ballroom, with its crystal chandeliers and ornate frescoes.
The ancient palace of the emperor stood as a testament to the grandeur of past civilizations, its ruins offering glimpses into history.
The sultan's palace was a masterpiece of Islamic architecture, with intricate tilework, soaring minarets, and lush inner courtyards.

senses.definition: a country house that has a large garden, particularly the one located in southern Europe or warm regions

examples.x: They spent their summer vacation in a beautiful villa overlooking the Mediterranean Sea, surrounded by lush gardens.
The family rented a villa in Tuscany, where they could enjoy the quiet countryside and local wines.
The villa was spacious, with several bedrooms, a private pool, and a large terrace perfect for evening dinners.
After retiring, they decided to move to a villa in the south of Spain, where the weather was warm year-round.
The villa had a charming, rustic design, with terracotta tiles and large windows that let in the natural light.

senses.definition: a small roofed building with open sides, usually in a garden

examples.x: The couple exchanged their wedding vows under a beautifully decorated gazebo in the garden.
Every summer, the family gathers for a picnic in the gazebo, enjoying the cool shade and scenic views.
The park's centerpiece is a large, ornate gazebo where musicians often perform concerts.
She loved to spend her afternoons reading in the gazebo, surrounded by the fragrance of blooming flowers.
The new gazebo in the backyard became the perfect spot for evening tea and watching the sunset.

senses.definition: a small house separated from a larger one where guests can stay

examples.x: The guest house nestled in the garden offered a tranquil retreat for visitors, with its cozy bedrooms and private patio.
The guest house at the bed and breakfast featured charming decor and personalized service, ensuring a memorable stay for guests.
Families rented out the guest house for reunions and gatherings, enjoying the convenience of having additional space and privacy.
Tourists opted to stay in the guest house to experience local hospitality and immerse themselves in the culture of the region.
Business travelers appreciated the convenience of the guest house, with its proximity to the conference center and shuttle service to the airport.

senses.definition: a building of small size, such as a shed or garage, that shares a wall and roof with a building of larger size

examples.x: The hikers built a simple lean-to with branches and leaves to protect themselves from the unexpected rain.
In the dense forest, they discovered an old lean-to that had been used by previous campers.
The farmer stored his tools and supplies in a makeshift lean-to attached to the barn.
Seeking shelter from the wind, they huddled under a rocky overhang that acted as a natural lean-to.
The children spent the afternoon constructing a lean-to in the backyard, pretending they were explorers in the wilderness.

senses.definition: a small house in the countryside, where people temporarily stay while participating in an outdoor activity such as climbing, skiing, etc.
a round hall or building that often has a rounded roof as well
a small, simple cottage often constructed from basic materials

examples.x: The fisherman lived in a wooden shanty by the riverbank.
Families in the village built shanties out of scraps for shelter.
We stayed in a cozy beach shanty during our vacation.
Despite its rustic appearance, the shanty had a certain charm.
A small vegetable garden thrived next to the shanty.

senses.definition: a place or building that is meant to provide protection against danger or bad weather

examples.x: The hikers found shelter under a rocky overhang during the storm.
The organization provides food and shelter to the homeless.
The refugees were taken to a nearby shelter for safety.
The soldiers constructed a shelter to rest for the night.

senses.definition: a large building consisting of several apartments, particularly in a poor neighborhood

examples.x: Despite its dilapidated appearance, the tenement served as a refuge for those struggling to make ends meet, offering a semblance of stability and belonging.

senses.definition: a type of traditional dwelling made from sun-dried bricks, commonly found in arid and warm regions

examples.x: The adobe house, with its thick earthen walls, stayed cool during the hot summer days and warm during the cold nights.
They decided to renovate the old adobe house, preserving its historic charm while adding modern amenities.
The village was known for its distinctive adobe houses, each one a testament to traditional building techniques.
Inside the adobe house, the rustic wooden beams and clay floors created a cozy and inviting atmosphere.
After touring the region, they fell in love with the simplicity and beauty of the adobe house they eventually purchased.

senses.definition: a place for living in, such as a house, apartment, etc.

examples.x: The cozy dwelling nestled in the countryside was the perfect retreat for those seeking solitude and tranquility.
The urban dwelling offered modern amenities and convenience, with easy access to public transportation and city attractions.
As night fell, the flickering lights in each dwelling illuminated the quiet neighborhood, signaling the end of another day.

senses.definition: a small secondary residence, typically located in a city or urban area, that is used as a temporary or occasional dwelling
a very tall building with many floors

examples.x: The city skyline was dotted with towering high-rises, each competing for prominence.
The neighborhood was undergoing rapid development, with new high-rises springing up every year.

senses.definition: a small separate building that is built near a main building that it belongs to, such as a shed or barn

examples.x: He keeps his bicycle and camping gear in the outbuilding on the property.
The family decided to turn the outbuilding into a guest house for visiting relatives.
The outbuilding was converted into a small workshop for woodworking projects.
They built an outbuilding to house their chickens and other farm animals.
She found some old furniture in the outbuilding that her grandparents had kept for years.

senses.definition: a small structure, gallery, or summerhouse that usually has an open side and provides an excellent view

examples.x: From the belvedere on the hill, they could see the entire valley spread out below them in breathtaking detail.
The estate's belvedere was a favorite spot for guests to gather and enjoy the sunset over the lake.
The architect designed a stunning belvedere that offered panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.
They enjoyed their morning coffee in the belvedere, listening to the birds and watching the sunrise.
The belvedere's open sides allowed a refreshing breeze to flow through, making it the perfect place to relax on a hot summer day.

senses.definition: a residential structure that is designed to float on water, providing a unique living environment with the possibility of mobility or stationary placement

examples.x: The family loves living in their floating home, enjoying the peaceful sound of the water every morning.
They spent the weekend on a floating home in the lake, relaxing and fishing.
Many people in coastal areas are opting for floating homes as an alternative to traditional houses.
The couple plans to retire on a floating home, cruising along the coastline for a few years.
There is a community of floating homes along the harbor, where people enjoy a unique lifestyle.

senses.definition: a house or shelter in the shape of a dome that is built from blocks of ice or hard snow

examples.x: In the Arctic, indigenous people have been building traditional igloos for generations to withstand harsh winter conditions.
The Inuit family constructed a cozy igloo using blocks of ice to protect themselves from the freezing Arctic winds.
Children in northern regions enjoy building miniature igloos out of snow during the winter months.
During the blizzard, the stranded mountaineers took refuge in an improvised igloo made from compacted snow blocks to stay warm and safe.

senses.definition: a sustainable and environmentally friendly home that is made primarily from natural and recycled materials, such as tires, bottles, and cans

examples.x: The couple decided to build an Earthship to live sustainably and reduce their environmental impact.
He toured an Earthship community, amazed by how the homes collect rainwater and generate their own power.
Living in an Earthship allows people to live off-grid, with little need for external utilities.
She planted a garden inside her Earthship to grow fresh vegetables year-round.

senses.definition: a circular portable tent, particularly used in Siberia and Mongolia

examples.x: The nomadic family moved their yurt to a new location, following the seasonal migration of their livestock.
Tourists can experience traditional Mongolian life by staying in a cozy yurt set up on the open steppe.
Despite its simple structure, the yurt was surprisingly warm and comfortable during the cold winter nights.
They decorated the inside of the yurt with colorful tapestries and traditional rugs, creating a welcoming atmosphere.

senses.definition: a boat designed for living in

examples.x: They spent their vacation on a houseboat cruising down the river.
The houseboat was anchored at the marina, offering a unique living experience.
She dreams of retiring on a houseboat and traveling from port to port.
The houseboat has all the amenities of a regular home, including a kitchen and bathroom.
They hosted a party on their houseboat, enjoying the sunset over the water.

senses.definition: an enclosed structure built among the branches of a tree to provide children with a place to play in

examples.x: She used the tree house as a quiet reading spot.
They built a cozy tree house in the backyard to enjoy reading and relaxing in the fresh air.
The couple designed their tree house with large windows, offering breathtaking views of the forest.
The kids had a blast decorating their tree house with fairy lights and colorful cushions.
The children spent the afternoon playing in their tree house, imagining it was a secret fort.

senses.definition: a tiny apartment that has only one main room
a multi-building or multi-unit development designed for residential purposes, often featuring shared amenities and services for the residents
a tall building primarily designed and constructed for residential purposes, typically housing multiple residential units or apartments within a vertical structure
a portable and temporary shelter used by nomadic communities, often made from animal hides, fabric, or woven materials
a traditional Javanese wooden house with a peaked roof and intricate carvings, commonly found in Java, Indonesia
a simple shelter or dwelling made by using a mixture of mud, clay, and other natural materials, commonly found in rural or traditional communities
a traditional mansion or large residence with intricate architectural designs, commonly found in South Asia
a traditional Moroccan house built around a central courtyard, often with ornate architecture and a peaceful atmosphere
a large and strong building that is protected against attacks, in which the royal family lives
a residence designed and built with a focus on environmental sustainability, energy efficiency, and the use of eco-friendly materials and technologies to minimize its impact on the environment

examples.x: Solar panels on the roof of the ecohouse provided much of the electricity needed for daily life.
The family chose to live in an ecohouse to reduce their carbon footprint and live more sustainably.
The use of recycled materials in the construction of the ecohouse made it an environmentally friendly option.
The ecohouse was designed with large windows to let in natural light, reducing the need for artificial lighting.
Many ecohouses are built in rural areas, offering a peaceful and sustainable way of living.

senses.definition: a building or a set of buildings for soldiers to live in

examples.x: The soldiers were assigned to newly built barracks, which provided much better living conditions than the old ones.
After a long day of training, the recruits returned to their barracks to rest and recuperate.
The historic barracks, now a museum, offer a glimpse into the daily lives of soldiers from the past.
The barracks were filled with the sounds of camaraderie as soldiers shared stories and jokes in their free time.
During the inspection, the commander praised the soldiers for maintaining such orderly and clean barracks.

senses.definition: a house, particularly found in New York City, that is built of, or with a front made of, a type of reddish-brown sandstone

examples.x: They recently purchased a charming brownstone in Brooklyn.
The historic district is famous for its row of elegant brownstones.
Her dream home was always a brownstone with a classic stoop.
He inherited the family brownstone located in the Upper West Side.
The renovation of the old brownstone took nearly a year to complete.

senses.definition: a small building with a high ceiling that is primarily used to provide a covered space for coaches and carriages when they are not in use

examples.x: The old carriage house was converted into a cozy guest cottage for visiting family and friends.
The family restored the carriage house to its original design, keeping the large doors and wooden beams.
The estate included a carriage house that was used to store vintage cars and other equipment.
They turned the carriage house into a studio, using the spacious area for painting and crafting.
The carriage house still had the tracks on the floor where the horses' carriages used to be stored.

senses.definition: a single-family house that is not connected to any other house, usually with its own yard or garden
